data.name: PWN Request / Poisoned Pipeline Execution
data.description: Attacker submits malicious PR that triggers workflow with elevated privileges
risks: Default workflow permissions set to WRITE
Default workflow permissions set to approve PR
controls: Require explicit approval/ disallow workflows from forks (VCS)
Default workflow permissions not set to approve PRs

data.name: Secret Exfiltration from Workflow
data.description: Attacker extracts secrets from CI/CD workflow execution

data.name: Abuse Credentials for VCS Access
data.description: Attacker uses stolen credentials to access VCS
risks: Credentials in public repos of org members

data.name: Imposter Commits
data.description: Attacker commits code impersonating another developer

data.name: Malicious Code Modification in Repository
data.description: Attacker with repository access directly modifies source code to inject backdoors, malicious logic, or vulnerabilities into the codebase
risks: Direct push to protected branches allowed
No detection of suspicious code patterns
controls: Mandatory code review by multiple reviewers
Minimal VCS permissions (principle of least privilege)

data.name: Malicious Execution in Workflow Context
data.description: Attacker executes malicious code within CI/CD workflow
risks: Using untrusted SW packages on runners
Using untrusted container / VM image for runners
controls: Automated maintainer reputation and hygiene checks

data.description: Attacker extracts secrets from CI/CD workflow execution

data.name: Mass Deletion of Repositories
data.description: Attacker deletes multiple repositories to cause data loss and operational disruption
risks: Insufficient access controls on critical repositories

data.name: Container Image Poisoning to Production
data.description: Attacker publishes malicious container image that gets deployed to production

data.name: Production Infrastructure Destruction
data.description: Attacker destroys production infrastructure, databases, storage, or configurations to cause severe operational disruption and data loss
risks: Overprivileged IAM roles allowing destructive actions
No backup and disaster recovery plan
Lack of deletion protection on critical resources
Insufficient access controls on critical infrastructure
No approval process for destructive operations
Backup data not isolated from production
